mn34:1.2: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling among the Vajjīs at Ukkacelā, on the bank of the Ganges River.
mn34:10.1: Just as that newborn calf, *bhikkhus*, just born, swept along by attachment to its mother, cut across the current of the Ganges and safely reached the far shore;
mn34:10.2: in the same way, *bhikkhus*, those *bhikkhus* who are followers of *dhamma* and followers by faith—they too will cut across the current of Māra and safely reach the far shore.
mn34:11.1: But I, *bhikkhus*, am skillful regarding this world, skillful regarding the other world, skillful regarding Māra’s domain, skillful regarding what is beyond Māra’s domain, skillful regarding death’s domain, skillful regarding what is beyond death’s domain. Whoever thinks that what I say should be listened to and believed, that will be for their welfare and *sukha* for a long time.”
mn34:12.10: a secure way to the attainment of *nibbāna*.
mn34:12.11: The current of the Evil One is cut,
mn34:12.15: The Shorter Cowherd is finished, the fourth.
mn34:12.2: Having said this, the Fortunate One then further said this, the Teacher:
mn34:12.4: have been well made clear by the one who knows;
mn34:12.5: and what has been reached by Māra,
mn34:12.6: and what has not been reached by death.
mn34:12.7: Having directly known the whole world,
mn34:12.8: by the fully awakened one who understands;
mn34:12.9: the door to the deathless stands open,
mn34:2.1: “Once in the past, *bhikkhus*, there was a cowherd from Magadha, one of poor discernment [*duppaññajātiko*]. In the last month of the rains, in the autumn season, without examining the near bank of the Ganges River, without examining the far bank, he drove the cattle across at a non-fording place to the northern bank, to the Suvidehans.
mn34:3.1: Then, *bhikkhus*, in midstream in the current of the Ganges River, the cattle spun around in circles and there they came to disaster and ruin.
mn34:3.2: What was the reason for that?
mn34:3.3: Because that cowherd from Magadha, *bhikkhus*, one of poor discernment [*duppaññajātiko*], in the last month of the rains, in the autumn season, without examining the near bank of the Ganges River, without examining the far bank, drove the cattle across at a non-fording place to the northern bank, to the Suvidehans.
mn34:3.4: In the same way, *bhikkhus*, whatever ascetics or brahmins are unskillful regarding this world, unskillful regarding the other world, unskillful regarding Māra’s domain, unskillful regarding what is beyond Māra’s domain, unskillful regarding death’s domain, unskillful regarding what is beyond death’s domain—whoever thinks that what they say should be listened to and believed, that will be for their harm and *dukkha* for a long time.
mn34:4.1: Once in the past, *bhikkhus*, there was a cowherd from Magadha, one of good discernment [*sappaññajātiko*]. In the last month of the rains, in the autumn season, having examined the near bank of the Ganges River, having examined the far bank, he drove the cattle across at the ford to the northern bank, to the Suvidehans.
mn34:5.1: First he drove across those bulls, herd sires, leaders of the herd.
mn34:5.10: What was the reason for that?
mn34:5.11: Because that cowherd from Magadha, *bhikkhus*, one of good discernment [*sappaññajātiko*], in the last month of the rains, in the autumn season, having examined the near bank of the Ganges River, having examined the far bank, drove the cattle across at the ford to the northern bank, to the Suvidehans.
mn34:5.12: In the same way, *bhikkhus*, whatever ascetics or brahmins are skillful regarding this world, skillful regarding the other world, skillful regarding Māra’s domain, skillful regarding what is beyond Māra’s domain, skillful regarding death’s domain, skillful regarding what is beyond death’s domain—whoever thinks that what they say should be listened to and believed, that will be for their welfare and *sukha* for a long time.
mn34:5.2: They cut across the current of the Ganges and safely reached the far shore.
mn34:5.3: Then he drove across the strong cattle, the trained cattle.
mn34:5.4: They too cut across the current of the Ganges and safely reached the far shore.
mn34:5.5: Then he drove across the young bulls and the young heifers.
mn34:5.6: They too cut across the current of the Ganges and safely reached the far shore.
mn34:5.7: Then he drove across the calves, thin and feeble.
mn34:5.8: They too cut across the current of the Ganges and safely reached the far shore.
mn34:5.9: Once in the past, *bhikkhus*, there was a newborn calf, just born, swept along by attachment to its mother. It too cut across the current of the Ganges and safely reached the far shore.
mn34:6.1: Just as those bulls, herd sires, leaders of the herd, *bhikkhus*, cut across the current of the Ganges and safely reached the far shore;
mn34:6.2: in the same way, *bhikkhus*, those *bhikkhus* who are *arahants*, with *āsavas* destroyed [*khīṇāsavā*], who have lived the holy life, done what had to be done, laid down the burden, reached their own goal, with the fetters of *bhava* entirely destroyed, completely liberated through final knowledge—they have cut across the current of Māra and safely gone to the far shore.
mn34:7.1: Just as those strong cattle, trained cattle, *bhikkhus*, cut across the current of the Ganges and safely reached the far shore;
mn34:7.2: in the same way, *bhikkhus*, those *bhikkhus* who, with the wearing away of the five lower fetters, are reborn spontaneously and attain final *nibbāna* there, not of a nature to return from that world—they too will cut across the current of Māra and safely reach the far shore.
mn34:8.1: Just as those young bulls and young heifers, *bhikkhus*, cut across the current of the Ganges and safely reached the far shore;
mn34:8.2: in the same way, *bhikkhus*, those *bhikkhus* who, with the wearing away of three fetters and with lust, aversion, and delusion weakened, are once-returners, and after coming back to this world only once, will make an end of *dukkha*—they too will cut across the current of Māra and safely reach the far shore.
mn34:9.1: Just as those calves, thin and feeble, *bhikkhus*, cut across the current of the Ganges and safely reached the far shore;
mn34:9.2: in the same way, *bhikkhus*, those *bhikkhus* who, with the wearing away of three fetters, are stream-enterers, not liable to downfall, fixed in destiny, headed for full awakening—they too will cut across the current of Māra and safely reach the far shore.
